titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to John Quinnell, 22 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Clerk of Union and Council, Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Patrick Carey to John Quinnell

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

correspAction.persName: Clerk of Union and Council, Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
22nd December 1915, Dear Sir, At a meeting of the Guardians on the 18th Inst. Mr
James J Coffey inquired how many insertions of
advertisements were given to the Newspapers; and in the
course of the discussion Mr John Murphy stated that I
forwarded his Paying Orders to Mr Quinnell. As Mr Murphy's
tender only has been accepted for the advertising of the
business of the Union and Council in certain papers I
shall send the advertisements to him in future and the
Paying Orders will be drawn in his favour and sent direct
to him. The Paying Orders hitherto, so far as advertising
in the Killarney Echo, were drawn in his favour. Yours faithfully,
Clerk of Union & Council. JB Quinnell Esq
E Harrington Esq
Tralee.
